name: alphaclaw
description: The ultimate setup harness for OpenClaw. Deploy in minutes. Stay running for months. No CLI required.

commit.message:
Persist Telegram topic agent overrides in synced config.

Propagate topic-level agentId values into openclaw topic mappings during Telegram sync and add regression tests, while including the current featured-model list updates in the same worktree commit.

Show unsaved model auth state and add GPT-5.4 featured option.

This keeps provider auth badges in an unsaved state until Save changes is applied, persists cleared API-key values by removing env vars, and includes GPT-5.4 alongside Codex 5.3 in featured/onboarding model defaults.

Add GPT-5.4 to featured and onboarding model defaults.

This updates featured model mapping and onboarding fallback options to include openai-codex/gpt-5.4 while keeping usage pricing coverage in sync.

Add channel create progress and delete-agent guard-rail coverage.

Exercise channel creation progress fallback/debounce/settlement behavior in frontend tests and verify delete-agent guard rails never attempt workspace deletion when deletion is rejected.

Refactor agent bindings channel rendering and align channel name prefill behavior.

Split agent bindings channel-item derivation into a dedicated hook, add an optional ChannelsCard custom row renderer, and clear create-channel name defaults when a provider already has configured accounts.

Centralize OpenClaw config writes and migrate phase one callsites.

This adds a shared write helper and updates agents plus Telegram workspace/config routes to use shared read/write config access for consistent path and serialization handling.

Deduplicate channel helper utilities across server and UI.

This centralizes shared channel/binding account helpers into reusable modules and updates all callsites to reduce duplication while preserving existing behavior.

Decompose agents service into focused domain modules.

This refactors the monolithic agents service into dedicated agents, bindings, channels, and shared utility modules while preserving behavior and existing route/service contracts.

Harden agent and channel operations and backfill critical tests.

This closes merge-blocking security and runtime gaps while reducing regression risk across account creation, pairing approvals, agent CRUD routes, and SSE operation events.

commit.message: fix: allow org-owned workspace repos during onboarding

Fix channel account listing with legacy configs and bump OpenClaw.

Skip unsupported channel providers when building configured channel accounts to prevent empty channel state regressions on existing deployments, and update the OpenClaw dependency to 2026.3.8.

Refine assigned-elsewhere channel list affordances.

Use the same caret style as the file browser for the agent panel toggle, and preserve dimmed channel labels where applicable for clearer ownership context.

Align channel ordering and complete channel operation flow updates.

This restores openclaw.json-based channel/account ordering in operation panels, keeps Telegram channel creates from forcing restart-required, and includes the latest channel operation transport/UI wiring and related route/service test updates.

Fix watchdog uptime tracking to only reflect actual gateway runtime.

Uptime now starts when the gateway reports "listening on" (via
onGatewayLaunch) and clears immediately on exit, crash, expected
restart, or stop — so the UI no longer shows stale uptime across
restarts.

Gate device pairing polling behind dashboard open action.

Avoid calling /api/devices continuously on General tab load by enabling device polling only after the user clicks Open on the OpenClaw dashboard card.

Stabilize channel creation restart sequencing and improve create modal feedback.

Run a controlled gateway restart before channel activation so env-backed tokens are available during plugin/channel reload, remove redundant create restart-required signaling, and update the channel modal to show phased loading states with medium-sized actions.

Revert "Finalize WhatsApp flows and agent/channel UX polish."

This reverts commit cacf9c7d2ac72bc76198c6ede6659b5441dc0833.

Finalize WhatsApp flows and agent/channel UX polish.

This aligns channel ordering with openclaw.json, adds QR login handling and restart/polling behavior fixes, and updates agent navigation so create/delete land on the expected agent routes.

Prevent add-agent sidebar action from auto-selecting the first agent.

Use a pending-create route flag so clicking the sidebar plus opens the create-agent modal after navigating to /agents without forcing a first-agent redirect.

Merge branch 'main' into feat/multi-agent-sidebar-ui

# Conflicts:
#	lib/server.js
#	lib/server/onboarding/workspace.js
#	tests/server/gateway.test.js

Remove redundant control-ui skill and consolidate into TOOLS.md.

The control-ui skill contradicted TOOLS.md (skill said "don't call APIs,
direct user to UI" while TOOLS.md said "don't deflect, execute yourself
first"). Merges the unique Google Workspace content (services table, gog
CLI commands, defaults) into TOOLS.md and removes the skill template,
installer, and all references. Keeps the legacy backfill check in
gateway.js for existing deployments.

Polish channel edit loading and agent channel visibility.

Use inline SecretInput loading state to avoid token-field layout shifts while fetching saved channel tokens, and hide channels owned by other agents from the current agent overview list.

Unify overflow menu close behavior across setup panels.

Move outside-click close handling into the shared OverflowMenu component and update menu callsites to use onClose, then switch Google Accounts add actions to the shared brand dropdown pattern.

Refine channel account flows and gateway restart signaling.

Unify channel add/edit UX with shared provider picker behavior, load real tokens only on edit, and surface restart-required only for token-affecting channel changes. Also harden gateway/watchdog lifecycle tracking by keying readiness on the gateway listening signal and suppressing duplicate-launch crash handling.

Unify channel operations UI and tighten webhook delivery routing.

This consolidates shared channel/pairing presentation, improves webhook and Gmail delivery targeting with agent-aware routing metadata, and standardizes openclaw.json reads through a shared config helper.
Consolidate git discipline and changes-committed instructions into AGENTS.md

Scope Telegram workspaces by account and agent while tightening pairing and token handling.

This keeps workspace bootstrap and TOOLS visibility aligned with routing, persists topic agent assignments on create, and prevents false-success pairing rejects or cleartext channel token exposure in the UI.

Adjust sidebar update button spacing.

Add top padding above the sidebar footer update action so the divider is evenly inset from the button.

Sync control UI origins during boot.

Reuse the managed setup URL resolver during startup so existing installs backfill gateway allowed origins on restart.

Sync control UI origins during boot.

Reuse the managed setup URL resolver during startup so existing installs backfill gateway allowed origins on restart.

Polish agent setup actions and onboarding pairing.

Remove per-agent model selection from agent creation, align the agent and channel action buttons with the current modal sizing, and restore onboarding pairing by passing account-aware pairing metadata through the welcome flow. Tidy the agent overview layout by moving pending pairings above manage actions and keeping unconfigured channels sorted last.

Refactor the agent bindings section into focused modules.

Split the agent channel bindings UI into a feature folder with dedicated helpers and state management so the overview keeps a thin render shell and the bindings flow follows the project’s index and hook structure.

Refactor the agent overview into self-contained cards.

Move the workspace, model, and manage panels into a dedicated feature folder so each card owns its own state and the overview follows the project’s index and hook-based component pattern.

Refine the agent overview and shared action menus.

Tighten the agent management flow by moving model controls into the overview, adding lazy workspace metadata, and making channel ownership and rebinding easier to navigate. Standardize the vertical overflow menus so channel, agent, and sidebar actions behave consistently across the setup UI.

Use the shared noisy JSON parser for pairings.

This keeps pairing-list parsing consistent with the rest of the server utilities while preserving the current account-aware pairing flow.

Embed agents in the main sidebar and streamline agent management.

This keeps multi-agent navigation in the primary menu, moves agent actions into a focused manage area, and hides channel-owned bot tokens from generic env editing while keeping pairing status in sync on agent screens.

Refine shared channel actions and pairing parsing.

Polish the shared channels UI with a subtle add action, fix loading and pairing badge refresh behavior, and make pairing list parsing resilient to the JSON output OpenClaw emits for Discord.

Expand multi-agent channel management and pairing flows.

Unify agent and general channel UIs around account-aware bindings, modal-based channel configuration, and per-account pairing state so Telegram and Discord behave consistently as multi-agent support expands.
Merge branch 'main' into feat/multi-agent-sidebar-ui

Notify on crash-loop recovery and silence gmail-serve child output.

Add a watchdog Telegram notification when the gateway recovers from a
crash loop, remove the redundant Trigger line from pending recovery
notices, and suppress noisy gmail watch serve child process logging.

Add multi-agent management UI with sidebar navigation

Introduce agent CRUD (create, read, update, delete) via a new /api/agents
REST layer backed by openclaw.json, and a sidebar-driven agents UI that
reuses the existing Home/Browse tab pattern with a third Agents tab.

Server: agents service with workspace/agentDir provisioning, route module,
and per-agent model config support (primary + fallbacks object shape).

Frontend: agent detail panel with Overview and Models sub-tabs, create/edit/
delete modals, per-agent model picker scoped to existing auth profiles, and
URL-driven agent selection (/agents/:agentId). Models tab gains an embedded
mode and optional agentId scoping.

Add GitHub Sponsors funding configuration.

Expose the sponsor button across the repository so users can support the project from repo pages.

Add contributing guide and scope sidebar update button.

Document how contributors can get started and submit changes, and only show the sidebar update CTA on the home tab.

Fix harness tool guidance and sync lockfile.

Ensure actionable requests are handled directly when tools are available, and keep the lockfile aligned with the released OpenClaw dependency tree.

Polish onboarding and setup UI details.

Refine Gmail setup behavior, import onboarding presentation, model page typography, and release guidance so the current beta better matches the latest setup workflow.

Improve setup workflows and webhook routing.

Polish session-driven delivery defaults, Gmail reconfiguration UX, model and doctor flows, and supporting frontend utilities so the beta release better matches the latest setup behavior.
Bump OpenClaw base to 2026.3.7

fix import onboarding and gateway config handling

Keep imported setups valid through onboarding by normalizing channel state, preserving approved secret values in the UI, and honoring dynamic gateway configuration during proxying and webhooks.

fix usage overview prompt token totals

Include cache write tokens in the overview prompt token calculation so the summary matches the full token usage shown elsewhere.

fix onboarding imports and model provider setup

Normalize imported config secrets and seed auth profiles so provider-backed models configure correctly. Expand the model picker with broader provider support, popular suggestions, and grouped searchable results.

fix runtime artifact bootstrap for imports

Move gog keyring file setup into the post-onboarding runtime artifact path so import flows are not blocked by startup-created files, while still restoring the keyring password env on every start. Clean up the onboarding UI by removing the unused agents route and tightening GitHub copy.

feat(ui): add onboarding pre-step and visual polish

- Extract 'Start fresh' vs 'Import' into a dedicated pre-step
- Add neon cyan hover effects to path selection cards
- Add pulsing animation to active step pill in header
- Display AlphaClaw version at bottom of onboarding flow

Merge branch 'repo-import'

# Conflicts:
#	package-lock.json
#	package.json

Harden onboarding import secret handling and error redaction.

Validate approved import secrets against server-side scan results, block path traversal during secret rewrites, and redact fine-grained GitHub tokens from onboarding error responses.

Improve onboarding import compatibility and review flow.

Restore imported repo onboarding behavior across GitHub verification, target repo setup, and fresh git initialization while adding a dedicated placeholder env review step before first gateway start.

Improve onboarding import flow for existing repos.

Split GitHub import into source and target repos, normalize imported OpenClaw config for AlphaClaw runtime expectations, and keep deployment-managed env/git behavior from mutating the source workspace unexpectedly.
Increase JSON body parser limit to 5mb for large file saves.

Add Drift Doctor Project Context truncation warnings.

Surface persistent truncation and near-limit warnings for injected workspace files so users can catch hidden instruction loss before it causes agent drift.

Add agent debug handoff for webhook failures.

Standardize the agent-send modal across Doctor and Webhooks, add a webhook history debug handoff with prefilled request context, and avoid false send errors when the agent command succeeds despite non-fatal noisy output.

Improve onboarding persistence and watchdog recovery handling.

Persist onboarding completion in a dedicated marker, allow Gmail setup to replace an incorrect project id, and tighten restart/crash handling so degraded gateway states surface correctly.

Improve setup auth syncing and gateway restart handling.

This tightens setup and onboarding flows across provider auth, model routing, and shared UI helpers while preventing duplicate or misclassified gateway restarts during forced refreshes.

Update gateway restart tests for start command.

Align the restart-flow tests with the current gateway restart behavior so the beta release is validated against the intended start path.

Improve usage overview metrics and gateway restart flow.

Add cache and per-turn summary metrics to the usage overview so recent usage is easier to interpret, and switch gateway restarts back to the normal start path so restarts recover reliably with refreshed environment settings.

Fix watchdog status after crash-loop recovery.

Clear the stuck crash-loop lifecycle once health checks succeed again and log a recovery event so the UI reflects recovery without requiring a manual repair or server restart.

Add model and auth profile management in setup UI.

Replace the Providers tab with a Models workflow that edits OpenClaw model config and auth profiles directly, including Codex OAuth support and targeted git-sync persistence for openclaw.json.

Refactor app shell into hooks and route adapters

Move the remaining app controller, browse navigation, and shell UI state out of app.js so the top-level entrypoint mostly composes routes and feature modules. This keeps the new route layer thin while preserving onboarding, restart flows, and browse behavior.

Refine Doctor prompt: specify exact injected file set, forbid locked file edits in fixPrompt

Clarify that only the fixed set of named workspace files (AGENTS.md,
SOUL.md, TOOLS.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md, HEARTBEAT.md, BOOTSTRAP.md)
are auto-injected — not all root .md files. Prevent fixPrompt from
suggesting edits to locked/managed files since they get overwritten on
boot. Remove spinner from run-in-progress message.

Add file-linked findings with inline previews, collapsible snippets, and independent scroll panes

Doctor findings now link to specific file lines in the viewer with
auto-scroll and highlight. Evidence sections render captured code
snippets with staleness detection, collapsible beyond 5 lines with a
gradient fade. The fix modal is simplified to just session picker and
instructions. The Doctor prompt now explains OpenClaw's auto-injection
of root .md files to prevent false orphan findings. App layout uses
independent scroll panes so Doctor scroll position is preserved when
navigating to the file viewer.

Improve Doctor heuristics, centralize localStorage keys, share session picker

- Workspace fingerprint now stores file sizes and scores modified content
  files by byte delta (small fix = low score, major rewrite = full weight),
  reducing false "meaningful changes" signals from Doctor-applied fixes.
- Drop yellow highlight from change label; always neutral gray.
- Centralize all localStorage keys in lib/public/js/lib/storage-keys.js
  with consistent alphaclaw.<area>.<purpose> naming; remove legacy keys.
- Extract useAgentSessions hook with caching and last-selection memory,
  shared by Doctor fix modal and Gmail setup wizard.
- Pass previously dismissed/fixed findings to the Doctor prompt so the
  model avoids re-suggesting resolved issues.
- Fix missing await on onComplete in fix modal that prevented the card
  list from reflecting the "fixed" status before the modal closed.

Refine Doctor UI: simplify drift bar, editable fix prompts, auto-mark fixed, friendly topic names.

Replace drift risk badges with factual change count and yellow highlight for meaningful changes.
Add editable prompt textarea in fix-card modal, auto-mark findings fixed after sending,
resolve Telegram topic friendly names in session picker, move priority badge left of title.

Add Drift Doctor workspace review flow.

This adds persisted Drift Doctor runs, findings, and UI review workflows so users can review workspace drift and act on advisory fixes without rerunning unchanged scans. It also isolates Doctor analysis from the main agent thread so internal review sessions stay separate from normal conversations.

releases.name: AlphaClaw 0.6.0
releases.body:
### Changes

* **Multi-agent management UI**: add sidebar-driven agent navigation with create, rename, and delete flows, per-agent overview cards (workspace, model, channels), and URL-driven agent selection (`/agents/:agentId`). Agents are now first-class in the primary sidebar alongside Home and Browse.
* **Multi-agent channel bindings**: bind Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, and webhook channels to individual agents with account-aware pairing, modal-based channel configuration, and per-account pairing state, so each agent can own distinct channel accounts.
* **Scoped Telegram workspaces**: scope Telegram workspace bootstrap and TOOLS visibility by account and agent, persist topic agent assignments on channel create, and prevent cleartext channel token exposure in the UI.
* **Agent-aware webhook and Gmail delivery routing**: route inbound webhook and Gmail delivery to the correct agent based on channel binding metadata.
* **Channel creation restart sequencing**: run a controlled gateway restart before channel activation so env-backed tokens are available during plugin/channel reload, and surface phased loading states in the create modal.
* **GPT-5.4 model defaults**: add GPT-5.4 to featured model options and onboarding defaults, and show unsaved model auth state in the provider cards.
* **Centralized OpenClaw config writes**: add a shared `writeOpenclawConfig` helper alongside the existing read helper and migrate agents and Telegram workspace config routes to use it for consistent path and serialization handling.
* **Overflow menu unification**: move outside-click close handling into the shared `OverflowMenu` component so channel, agent, and sidebar action menus behave consistently across all setup panels.
* **Bump OpenClaw base to 2026.3.8**.

### Fixes

* **Control UI origin sync**: reuse the managed setup URL resolver during startup so existing installs backfill gateway allowed origins on restart, fixing "origin not allowed" errors on fresh Railway template deploys. Fixes #5.
* **Watchdog uptime tracking**: uptime now starts when the gateway reports "listening on" and clears immediately on exit, crash, expected restart, or stop, so the UI no longer shows stale uptime across restarts.
* **Device pairing polling**: gate `/api/devices` polling behind the user clicking Open on the dashboard card, avoiding continuous polling on General tab load.
* **Legacy channel account listing**: skip unsupported channel providers when building configured channel accounts to prevent empty channel state regressions on existing deployments.
* **Onboarding: allow org-owned workspace repos**: fix repository validation during onboarding to accept organization-owned repos, not just user-owned ones. (#9) Thanks @tokudu.

### Internal

* Decompose the monolithic agents service into focused domain modules (`agents`, `bindings`, `channels`, `shared`).
* Refactor the agent overview and bindings UI into self-contained feature folders with dedicated hooks.
* Deduplicate channel helper utilities across server and UI into reusable modules.
* Harden agent and channel operations with security and runtime gap coverage and backfill critical tests (account creation, pairing approvals, agent CRUD routes, SSE operation events, channel create progress, delete-agent guard rails).

### Contributors

* @tokudu

releases.name: v0.5.6
releases.body:
## What's New
- Sync `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` during boot, using the same setup URL fallback chain as the managed AGENTS/TOOLS bootstrap so existing installs backfill the Control UI origin on restart.
- Improve crash-loop recovery notices and reduce noisy `gmail-serve` child-process output during watchdog-managed operation.

## Verification
- `npm test`

readme_html:
Platform: AlphaClaw currently targets Docker/Linux deployments. macOS local development is not yet supported.

Features

  • Setup UI: Password-protected web dashboard for onboarding, configuration, and day-to-day management.
  • Guided Onboarding: Step-by-step setup wizard — model selection, provider credentials, GitHub repo, channel pairing.
  • Gateway Manager: Spawns, monitors, restarts, and proxies the OpenClaw gateway as a managed child process.
  • Watchdog: Crash detection, crash-loop recovery, auto-repair (openclaw doctor --fix), and Telegram/Discord notifications.
  • Channel Orchestration: Telegram and Discord bot pairing, credential sync, and a guided wizard for splitting Telegram into multi-threaded topic groups as your usage grows.
  • Webhooks: Named webhook endpoints with per-hook transform modules, request logging, and payload inspection.
  • Google Workspace: OAuth integration for Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Tasks, Contacts, and Meet, plus guided Gmail watch setup with Google Pub/Sub topic, subscription, and push endpoint handling.
  • File Explorer: Browser-based workspace explorer with file visibility, inline edits, diff view, and Git-aware sync for quick fixes without SSH.
  • Prompt Hardening: Ships anti-drift bootstrap prompts (AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md) injected into your agent's system prompt on every message — enforcing safe practices, commit discipline, and change summaries out of the box.
  • Git Sync: Automatic hourly commits of your OpenClaw workspace to GitHub with configurable cron schedule. Combined with prompt hardening, every agent action is version-controlled and auditable.
  • Version Management: In-place updates for both AlphaClaw and OpenClaw with changelog review and one-click apply.
  • Codex OAuth: Built-in PKCE flow for OpenAI Codex CLI model access.

Why AlphaClaw

  • Zero to production in one deploy: Railway/Render templates ship a complete stack — no manual gateway setup.
  • Self-healing: Watchdog detects crashes, enters repair mode, relaunches the gateway, and notifies you.
  • Everything in the browser: No SSH, no config files to hand-edit, no CLI required after first deploy.
  • Stays out of the way: AlphaClaw manages infrastructure; OpenClaw handles the AI.

No Lock-in. Eject Anytime.

AlphaClaw simply wraps OpenClaw, it's not a dependency. Remove AlphaClaw and your agent keeps running. Nothing proprietary, nothing to migrate.

Quick Start

Deploy (recommended)

Set SETUP_PASSWORD at deploy time and visit your deployment URL. The welcome wizard handles the rest.

Local / Docker

npm install @chrysb/alphaclaw
npx alphaclaw start

Or with Docker:

FROM node:22-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git curl procps cron && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json ./
RUN npm install --omit=dev
ENV PATH="/app/node_modules/.bin:$PATH"
ENV ALPHACLAW_ROOT_DIR=/data
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["alphaclaw", "start"]

Setup UI

Tab What it manages
General Gateway status, channel health, pending pairings, Google Workspace, repo sync schedule, OpenClaw dashboard
Browse File explorer for workspace visibility, inline edits, diff review, and Git-backed sync
Usage Token summaries, per-session and per-agent cost and token breakdown
Watchdog Health monitoring, crash-loop status, auto-repair toggle, notifications toggle, event log, live log tail
Providers AI provider credentials (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral, Voyage, Groq, Deepgram) and model selection
Envars Environment variables — view, edit, add — with gateway restart prompts
Webhooks Webhook endpoints, transform modules, request history, payload inspection, including Gmail watch delivery flows

CLI

Command Description
alphaclaw start Start the server (Setup UI + gateway manager)
alphaclaw git-sync -m "message" Commit and push the OpenClaw workspace
alphaclaw telegram topic add --thread <id> --name <text> Register a Telegram topic mapping
alphaclaw version Print version
alphaclaw help Show help

Architecture

graph TD
    subgraph AlphaClaw
        UI["Setup UI<br/><small>Preact + htm + Wouter</small>"]
        WD["Watchdog<br/><small>Crash recovery · Notifications</small>"]
        WH["Webhooks<br/><small>Transforms · Request logging</small>"]
        UI --> API
        WD --> API
        WH --> API
        API["Express Server<br/><small>JSON APIs · Auth · Proxy</small>"]
    end

    API -- "proxy" --> GW["OpenClaw Gateway<br/><small>Child process · 127.0.0.1:18789</small>"]
    GW --> DATA["ALPHACLAW_ROOT_DIR<br/><small>.openclaw/ · .env · logs · SQLite</small>"]

Watchdog

The built-in watchdog monitors gateway health and recovers from failures automatically.

Capability Details
Health checks Periodic openclaw health with configurable interval
Crash detection Listens for gateway exit events
Crash-loop detection Threshold-based (default: 3 crashes in 300s)
Auto-repair Runs openclaw doctor --fix --yes, relaunches gateway
Notifications Telegram and Discord alerts for crashes, repairs, and recovery
Event log SQLite-backed incident history with API and UI access

Environment Variables

Variable Required Description
SETUP_PASSWORD Yes Password for the Setup UI
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN Auto Gateway auth token (auto-generated if unset)
GITHUB_TOKEN Yes GitHub PAT for workspace repo
GITHUB_WORKSPACE_REPO Yes GitHub repo for workspace sync (e.g. owner/repo)
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN Optional Telegram bot token
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN Optional Discord bot token
WATCHDOG_AUTO_REPAIR Optional Enable auto-repair on crash (true/false)
WATCHDOG_NOTIFICATIONS_DISABLED Optional Disable watchdog notifications (true/false)
PORT Optional Server port (default 3000)
ALPHACLAW_ROOT_DIR Optional Data directory (default /data)
TRUST_PROXY_HOPS Optional Trust proxy hop count for correct client IP

Security Notes

AlphaClaw is a convenience wrapper — it intentionally trades some of OpenClaw's default hardening for ease of setup. You should understand what's different:

Area What AlphaClaw does Trade-off
Setup password All gateway access is gated behind a single SETUP_PASSWORD. Brute-force protection is built in (exponential backoff lockout). Simpler than OpenClaw's pairing code flow, but the password must be strong.
One-click pairing Channel pairings (Telegram/Discord) can be approved from the Setup UI instead of the CLI. No terminal access required, but anyone with the setup password can approve pairings.
Auto CLI approval The first CLI device pairing is auto-approved so you can connect without a second screen. Subsequent requests appear in the UI. Removes the manual pairing step for the initial CLI connection.
Query-string tokens Webhook URLs support ?token=<WEBHOOK_TOKEN> for providers that don't support Authorization headers. Warnings are shown in the UI. Tokens may appear in server logs and referrer headers. Use header auth when your provider supports it.
Gateway token OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN is auto-generated and injected into the environment so the proxy can authenticate with the gateway. The token lives in the .env file on the server — standard for managed deployments but worth noting.

If you need OpenClaw's full security posture (manual pairing codes, no query-string tokens, no auto-approval), use OpenClaw directly without AlphaClaw.

Development

npm install
npm test                # Full suite (90 tests)
npm run test:watchdog   # Watchdog-focused suite (14 tests)
npm run test:watch      # Watch mode
npm run test:coverage   # Coverage report

Requirements: Node.js ≥ 22.12.0

License

MIT
